message.reasoning_content: We are asked to evaluate whether the provided source could concretely improve one of Joni's non-core modules. The source is a Zenodo record about "Governed Viability and Regret under Operator Drift: A Pseudospectral Constructibility Theory". The abstract mentions a unified theory of viability and regret for governed dynamical systems, with pseudospectral viability margin defined via resolvent norm supremum. It seems highly theoretical, dealing with dynamical systems, perturbations, regret. It doesn't immediately mention any practical method, tool, or technique that could be applied to the specific modules like semantics-measurement, conflict-qualifier, etc. Those modules are about semantic embeddings, conflict kind labeling, reading sources, emergence strengthening, method-trialing. The source's content is about a mathematical theory of viability and regret in dynamical systems, likely from control theory or similar. No direct mapping to any of the listed modules appears without stretching interpretation. So it's not applicable.
